rationale: The citing paper’s main contribution is a Bayesian nonparametric treatment of heterogeneity and distributional assumptions in marketing models, while the cited paper introduces a Bayesian nonparametric (GP-based) heterogeneity framework and documents bias from restrictive heterogeneity assumptions. This thematic/methodological overlap makes the citation plausibly substantive rather than incidental. The reference is also an exact DOI match (10.1177/0022243719874047).
